docs.title: arrow
docs.text:
The missing interface for Arch.

Another Ridiculously Readable Overlay Wrapper.

arrow makes Arch accessible — whether you're installing packages, setting up a desktop environment, or taking your first steps after a fresh install.

arrow add firefox neovim       # install packages
arrow del vlc                  # remove + clean orphans
arrow upgrade                  # full system upgrade
arrow setup gnome              # install and configure GNOME
arrow howto                    # interactive post-install guide
arrow doctor                   # check system health

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
Arch Linux is one of the most powerful and transparent Linux distributions — but it asks a lot from newcomers. The wiki is excellent, but it's a lot to absorb. Flags like -Syu, -Rns, and -Qdtq are hard to remember. Setting up audio, Bluetooth, or a desktop environment still requires cobbling together several wiki pages.

arrow is a single tool that covers the full journey:

  • Day zero — guided post-install walkthrough via howto
  • Day to day — plain English package management without memorising pacman flags
  • As you grow — setup commands for common configurations (desktop, audio, printing…)
  • System health — doctor checks mirrors, orphans, cache, and more

Everything arrow does is a direct, transparent call to pacman, systemctl, or other standard tools — no hidden state, no magic.

docs.title: Package management
docs.text:
arrow wraps pacman with memorable commands. Under the hood it's still pacman — you're not giving anything up.

arrow pacman What it does arrow add firefox pacman -S firefox Install a package arrow del vlc pacman -Rns vlc Remove + clean deps arrow search python pacman -Ss python Search repos arrow upgrade pacman -Syu Full upgrade arrow orphans pacman -Qdtq List unused deps arrow purge pacman -Rns $(…) Remove all orphans

Full command reference

docs.title: Setup & configuration
docs.text:
(coming soon)

arrow setup handles the things that send newcomers to the wiki.

arrow howto                    # interactive post-install walkthrough
arrow setup gnome              # install GNOME and enable GDM
arrow setup kde                # install KDE Plasma
arrow setup hyprland           # install Hyprland (Wayland)
arrow setup audio              # configure PipeWire
arrow setup bluetooth          # enable and configure Bluetooth
arrow setup printer            # CUPS + common drivers
arrow mirrors                  # refresh mirror list with reflector
arrow doctor                   # system health check

See the full roadmap

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oporpino/arrow/main/install.sh | bash

Full installation guide

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to this project are documented here. Format follows Keep a Changelog. Versioning follows Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Commands: add, del, search, info, files, own, deps
  • System commands: update, upgrade, clean, orphans, purge
  • Query commands: list, history
  • AUR subcommand: aur add, aur search, aur upgrade (delegates to yay/paru)
  • Shell completion for bash, zsh, and fish
  • Man page (man arrow)
  • install.sh one-liner with version pinning (ARROW_VERSION=vx.x.x)
  • Makefile with build, install, uninstall, clean-dist targets
  • Colour-aware output (ANSI codes disabled when stdout is not a TTY)
  • Command aliases: rm/remove, find/s, up, ls, log

docs.title: Shell Completion
docs.text: arch ships with completion scripts for bash, zsh, and fish. They are installed automatically by make install.

docs.title: What gets completed
docs.text:
• Top-level commands (add, del, search, upgrade, …)
  • Installed package names for commands that need them (del, info, files, deps)
  • aur subcommands (add, search, upgrade)
  • clean option (--all)
  • File paths for own

docs.title: bash
docs.text:
The completion file is installed to /usr/share/bash-completion/completions/arch.

If it doesn't activate immediately, reload your shell or source it manually:

source /usr/share/bash-completion/completions/arch

Ensure bash-completion is installed:

arch add bash-completion

docs.title: zsh
docs.text:
The completion file (_arch) is installed to /usr/share/zsh/site-functions/.

Add the following to your ~/.zshrc if completions don't activate automatically:

autoload -Uz compinit && compinit

Make sure /usr/share/zsh/site-functions is in your $fpath (it is by default on Arch).

docs.title: fish
docs.text:
The completion file is installed to /usr/share/fish/vendor_completions.d/arch.fish.

fish sources completions automatically — no configuration needed. Start a new shell session and press Tab after arch.

docs.title: Manual activation
docs.text:
If you installed to a custom PREFIX, completions land in $PREFIX/share/…. Point your shell to them:

bashzshfish 
# add to ~/.bashrc
source "$PREFIX/share/bash-completion/completions/arch"

# add to ~/.zshrc, before compinit
fpath=("$PREFIX/share/zsh/site-functions" $fpath)
autoload -Uz compinit && compinit

# fish respects XDG_DATA_DIRS
set -gx XDG_DATA_DIRS "$PREFIX/share" $XDG_DATA_DIRS

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions are welcome! Here's how to get involved.

docs.title: Reporting bugs
docs.text:
Open an issue at github.com/oporpino/arrow/issues. Please include:

  • Your Arch Linux version / kernel
  • The exact command you ran
  • The full output (including any error messages)

docs.title: Submitting a pull request
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository and create a branch from main
  2. Make your changes inside src/
  3. Run a syntax check: bash -n dist/arrow after make build
  4. If adding a command, add completions for all three shells
  5. Update docs/ if the change affects user-facing behaviour
  6. Open a pull request with a clear description

docs.title: Project structure
docs.text:
src/
  version.sh          version constant and repo URL
  colors.sh           ANSI colour definitions (no-op when not a TTY)
  helpers.sh          output functions (_info, _ok, _warn, _err, _die)
  commands/
    packages.sh       add, del, search, info, files, own, deps
    system.sh         update, upgrade, clean, orphans, purge
    query.sh          list, history
    aur.sh            aur subcommand
  help.sh             cmd_help, cmd_version
  main.sh             dispatcher + main "$@"
completions/
  arrow.bash           bash completion
  _arrow               zsh completion (_arrow function)
  arrow.fish           fish completion
man/
  arrow.1              troff man page
docs/                 MkDocs source (this site)

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
make build      # → dist/arrow (single concatenated binary)
make install    # installs to /usr/local (or PREFIX)
make uninstall  # removes all installed files
make clean-dist # deletes dist/

docs.title: Code style
docs.text:
• Pure bash, no external tools beyond pacman, sudo, and standard coreutils
  • All functions named cmd_* for commands, _* for internal helpers
  • New commands follow the pattern in src/commands/packages.sh
  • Colours are defined in src/colors.sh and never hardcoded elsewhere

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
arrow's goal is to cover the full Arch Linux experience — from a fresh install to a fully configured daily driver — through a single, friendly CLI.

This page tracks what's planned. Contributions are very welcome.

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
Every arrow command that changes the system follows the same pattern:

  1. Show what it's about to do, in plain language
  2. Ask for confirmation before executing anything
  3. Report what happened after

arrow is an assistant, not an autopilot. The user stays in control and always knows what's happening.

$ arrow setup gnome

  :: The following packages will be installed:
     gnome gnome-extra gdm xorg-server (and 87 dependencies)

  :: The following services will be enabled:
     gdm.service

  Proceed? [y/N]

docs.title: arrow howto
docs.text:
An interactive guide for common tasks — especially useful right after a fresh Arch install. Instead of searching the wiki, arrow howto walks you through step by step, shows each command before running it, and asks for confirmation at every step.

arrow howto                    # browse all available guides
arrow howto post-install       # checklist after a fresh install
arrow howto locale             # set language and keyboard layout
arrow howto timezone           # configure timezone and clock sync
arrow howto user               # create a user, set up sudo
arrow howto mirrors            # pick the fastest mirrors
arrow howto aur                # install an AUR helper

Each guide explains why — not just what to run.

docs.title: arrow setup
docs.text:
Handles configurations that typically require multiple wiki pages and manual steps. Each subcommand shows what it will do and asks before touching the system.

arrow setup gnome              # install GNOME and enable GDM
arrow setup kde                # install KDE Plasma and SDDM
arrow setup xfce               # install XFCE and LightDM
arrow setup hyprland           # install Hyprland (Wayland compositor)
arrow setup sway               # install Sway (Wayland)
arrow setup audio              # configure PipeWire + WirePlumber
arrow setup bluetooth          # install bluez, enable service
arrow setup printer            # CUPS + common drivers
arrow setup nvidia             # install drivers, configure Xorg/Wayland
arrow setup fonts              # noto, nerd fonts, CJK support

docs.title: arrow mirrors
docs.text:
Refresh the pacman mirror list using reflector, keeping only the fastest mirrors for your region.

arrow mirrors                  # auto-detect country and refresh
arrow mirrors --country BR     # specify country

docs.title: arrow doctor
docs.text:
A system health check that surfaces common issues and suggests fixes — each with a confirmation prompt before acting.

Checks:

  • Outdated packages
  • Orphaned packages
  • Package cache size
  • Mirror latency and freshness
  • Failed systemd services
  • Disk usage on key mountpoints
  • Pacman lock file left over from a crash

docs.title: AUR without helpers (later)
docs.text: Currently arrow aur delegates to yay or paru. A future version will handle AUR packages natively — cloning the PKGBUILD, showing a diff for review, running makepkg, and installing via pacman. No external AUR helper required.

docs.title: add with AUR fallback (later)
docs.text:
When arrow add <pkg> doesn't find a match in the official repos, it will automatically search the AUR and offer to install from there — so users don't need to know the distinction.

$ arrow add spotify

  :: spotify not found in official repositories.
     Found in AUR: spotify 1.2.13
     Install from AUR? [y/N]

docs.title: Ideas under consideration
docs.text:
• arrow pin <pkg> — prevent a package from being upgraded (via IgnorePkg)
  • arrow log <pkg> — filter history for a specific package
  • arrow backup — export the list of explicitly installed packages
  • arrow restore — reinstall from a backup list
  • arrow news — show recent Arch Linux news before upgrades

Want to help?

Open an issue or pull request at github.com/oporpino/arrow. New commands follow the pattern in src/commands/ — each module is a focused, self-contained file.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: A full reference of every arch command and its pacman equivalent.

docs.title: Packages
docs.text: Command Description pacman add Install packages -S del Remove packages -Rns search Search repositories -Ss info Package details -Si / -Qi files List owned files -Ql own Find owner of a file -Qo deps Dependency tree pactree

docs.title: System
docs.text: Command Description pacman update Sync databases -Sy upgrade Upgrade system -Syu clean Clean package cache -Sc / -Scc orphans List orphaned packages -Qdtq purge Remove all orphans -Rns $(…)

docs.title: Query
docs.text: Command Description list List installed packages history Show pacman log entries

docs.title: AUR
docs.text: Command Description aur add Install from AUR aur search Search the AUR aur upgrade Upgrade AUR packages

docs.title: AUR Commands
docs.text:
arch aur delegates to the first available AUR helper on your system. Supported helpers (checked in order):

  1. yay
  2. paru

If neither is installed, arch aur will exit with an error.

Install an AUR helper

arch add base-devel git
git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git /tmp/yay
cd /tmp/yay && makepkg -si

docs.title: aur add
docs.text:
Install one or more packages from the Arch User Repository.

arch aur add <package> [package2 …]

Examples:

arch aur add visual-studio-code-bin
arch aur add spotify ttf-ms-fonts

docs.title: aur search
docs.text:
Search the AUR for packages matching a term.

arch aur search <term>

Examples:

arch aur search spotify
arch aur search "discord"

docs.title: aur upgrade
docs.text:
Upgrade all installed AUR packages.

arch aur upgrade

Example:

arch aur upgrade

Note

To upgrade both official and AUR packages in one step, run: 

arch upgrade       # official repositories
arch aur upgrade   # AUR packages

docs.title: add
docs.text:
Install one or more packages from the sync repositories.

arch add <package> [package2 …]

Examples:

arch add firefox
arch add git base-devel neovim ripgrep fd

pacman equivalent: pacman -S <package>

docs.title: del
docs.text:
Remove packages along with their now-unneeded dependencies and configuration files.

arch del <package> [package2 …]

Examples:

arch del gimp
arch del vlc ffmpeg

Aliases: rm, remove

pacman equivalent: pacman -Rns <package>

Info

Unlike a plain pacman -R, arch del also removes orphaned dependencies (-s) and configuration files (-n), leaving a cleaner system.

docs.title: search
docs.text:
Search the sync databases for packages matching a term.

arch search <term>

Examples:

arch search python
arch search "video editor"

Aliases: find, s

pacman equivalent: pacman -Ss <term>

docs.title: info
docs.text:
Show detailed information about a package. Checks the sync database first; if the package is not found there, checks the local (installed) database.

arch info <package>

Examples:

arch info firefox
arch info base

pacman equivalent: pacman -Si <package> / pacman -Qi <package>

docs.title: files
docs.text:
List all files installed by a package.

arch files <package>

Examples:

arch files bash
arch files neovim

pacman equivalent: pacman -Ql <package>

docs.title: own
docs.text:
Find which installed package owns a given file.

arch own <file>

Examples:

arch own /usr/bin/git
arch own /etc/pacman.conf

pacman equivalent: pacman -Qo <file>

docs.title: deps
docs.text:
Display the full dependency tree of a package. Uses pactree if available; falls back to pacman -Si otherwise.

arch deps <package>

Examples:

arch deps firefox
arch deps python

Tip

Install pactree for a richer tree view: 

arch add pacman-contrib

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all installed packages. Optionally filter by a search term.

arch list [filter]

Alias: ls

Examples:

arch list               # all installed packages
arch list python        # packages whose name contains "python"
arch list | wc -l       # count installed packages

pacman equivalent: pacman -Q

docs.title: history
docs.text:
Show recent pacman log entries (installs, removals, upgrades).

arch history [n]
  • n — number of entries to show (default: 20)

Alias: log

Examples:

arch history        # last 20 operations
arch history 50     # last 50 operations
arch history 5      # just the last 5

Example output:

[2025-06-01T12:34:56+0000] [ALPM] installed firefox (126.0-1)
[2025-06-01T12:35:10+0000] [ALPM] upgraded neovim (0.9.5-1 -> 0.10.0-1)
[2025-06-01T14:00:01+0000] [ALPM] removed vlc (3.0.21-1)

Reads from /var/log/pacman.log.

docs.title: update
docs.text:
Synchronise the local package databases with the upstream mirrors.

arch update

pacman equivalent: pacman -Sy

Warning

Running update without upgrade can leave your system in a partially updated state. Prefer arch upgrade for day-to-day maintenance.

docs.title: upgrade
docs.text:
Synchronise databases and upgrade all installed packages to their latest versions.

arch upgrade

Alias: up

pacman equivalent: pacman -Syu

Example:

arch upgrade

docs.title: clean
docs.text:
Remove outdated package versions from the local package cache to free disk space.

arch clean [--all]
Flag Behaviour (none) Keeps one cached version per package; removes the rest --all Wipes the entire cache

Examples:

arch clean        # light clean
arch clean --all  # full wipe

pacman equivalent: pacman -Sc / pacman -Scc

docs.title: orphans
docs.text:
List packages that were installed as dependencies but are no longer required by any installed package.

arch orphans

pacman equivalent: pacman -Qdtq

Example output:

Orphaned packages:
lib32-gcc-libs
perl-error
python-chardet

Use arch purge to remove them all at once.

docs.title: purge
docs.text:
Remove all orphaned packages (as reported by arch orphans) in a single step.

arch purge

pacman equivalent: pacman -Rns $(pacman -Qdtq)

Routine maintenance

Running these two commands periodically keeps your system lean: 

arch purge    # remove orphans
arch clean    # trim the cache

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Arch Linux or an Arch-based distribution (Manjaro, EndeavourOS, Garuda, etc.)
  • pacman must be installed (it is on every Arch system by default)
  • curl, tar, and make — installed automatically if missing

docs.title: One-liner
docs.text:
The fastest way to install the latest release:

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oporpino/arrow/main/install.sh | bash

This will:

  1. Detect your system and verify pacman is available
  2. Install any missing build dependencies via pacman
  3. Download the source code
  4. Build a single self-contained binary
  5. Install the binary, shell completions, and the man page

docs.title: Install a specific version
docs.text:
Pin to a release tag with the ARROW_VERSION variable:

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oporpino/arrow/main/install.sh \
  | ARROW_VERSION=v1.0.0 bash

Find all available versions on the releases page.

docs.title: Custom install prefix
docs.text:
Install without sudo by specifying a custom PREFIX:

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oporpino/arrow/main/install.sh \
  | PREFIX=~/.local bash

Then make sure ~/.local/bin is in your $PATH:

# add to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, or ~/.config/fish/config.fish
export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/.local/bin"

docs.title: From source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/oporpino/arrow
cd arrow
make build          # produces dist/arrow
sudo make install   # installs to /usr/local

Available make targets:

Target Description make build Compile sources into dist/arrow make install Install binary, completions, and man page make uninstall Remove all installed files make clean-dist Delete the dist/ directory

docs.title: Uninstall
docs.text:
sudo make uninstall

Or with a custom prefix:

make uninstall PREFIX=~/.local

docs.title: Your first commands
docs.text:
After installing, run arch help to see all available commands:

arch help

docs.title: Install a package
docs.text:
arch add firefox

Install multiple packages at once:

arch add git base-devel neovim ripgrep

docs.title: Remove a package
docs.text:
arch del removes the package and any dependencies that are no longer needed — no leftover cruft:

arch del gimp

docs.title: Search for packages
docs.text: arch search video editor

docs.title: Keep your system up to date
docs.text: arch upgrade

docs.title: System hygiene
docs.text:
Over time, Arch systems accumulate orphaned packages (dependencies of packages you've removed). Check for them regularly:

arch orphans

Remove them all at once:

arch purge

Clean the package cache to free disk space:

arch clean         # keeps one version of each package
arch clean --all   # wipes everything from the cache

docs.title: Useful inspection commands
docs.text:
# what do you know about this package?
arch info htop

# what files does a package install?
arch files bash

# which package owns this file?
arch own /usr/bin/python

# what has pacman been doing lately?
arch history
arch history 50   # last 50 entries

docs.title: AUR packages
docs.text:
If you have yay or paru installed, you can manage AUR packages too:

arch aur add visual-studio-code-bin
arch aur search spotify
arch aur upgrade

Tip

Don't have an AUR helper yet? Install one first: 

arch add base-devel git
git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git /tmp/yay
cd /tmp/yay && makepkg -si
Then arch aur add anything-from-the-aur just works.

docs.title: Aliases
docs.text:
Many commands have short aliases:

Alias Command rm, remove del find, s search up upgrade ls list log history
